transcript: Well, it’s only three-thirty.Uh, 9-4-3-[redacted], 9-4-3-[redacted].I need to get some contact numbers on musicians, so… And I know this is not really the right number but this is the number they gave me.9-4-3-[redacted], 9-4-3-[redacted].So, I don’t know—I don’t know what’s going on.
Alright, yeah. I don’t know if you guys over there are— Well, I know a lotta musicians are on drugs. But, uh, when you say, “four o’clock”, do you mean three-thirty that you close?
Our hours are 10 AM to 4 PM.
Yeah, well… 3:30 I called—you were closed. Now, can you get me a—
I said I called 3:30—you weren’t there!
I got my producer and director here—right by me! Now… You wanna try again? I need to talk to musicians.
”Why” I have to talk to musicians? Sir, how long have you been there?
I’ve been here for ‘bout half an hour and I’m tryin’ to help you—
I said, “How many years have you been there?”
Oh, gee whiz! I’ve only been here 20. Now get me to where I can get these names and contact numbers!
Tom Hopkins and, um, James Burton.
Don’t have any members by that name.
You know what!? Get me your supervisor! Lady, you’re knocking me out with your funniness. Get me your supervisor. Get me your boss. Now.I’ve had people fired there that’s been there 10 years!
Get me your— Get me your supervisor now!
There’s no one here right now.

Well you know what, lady? You must be on the rag right now. So you better get me James Burton—and you know that he’s listed. [stammers] [exasperated sigh]You know, you—you’re tryin’ to be funny. And the only thing is: your voice is on tape. So even though I don’t have your name, you’re still fired! Now get me somebody now! So help me! And then [breaks up, perhaps, “gimme a few minutes”]. Watch yourself.
We have no members by that name.
James Burton! Who— [laughs] Who plays the Elvis.
Uh, it— D’you know what!? James Burton. That’s right!
Wha—? Alright. Now. [unclear, perhaps, “Shall I do it?”]. I’ll call James at home. You wanna give me your name or you too scared for that? First and last!
Christine Ward. I’ll call James and [unclear, perhaps, “have you fired”]. And tell him that you don’t—
—have him listed as a member. And let me see how long you’re gonna be there. Uh… I assume that you’re a musician.
Can I help you with anything else?
I said, “I assume—“ Lemme tell you somethin’. We already have your home number.

But you sound like a woman. You sound like a woman. But we— we’ll— we can’t help that. Now! What I’m gonna do, again: are you gonna get me the name, are you gonna get me the numbers, or are you gonna still play games? And then I’ll get to the CEO and see how long you’re gonna be there.

If you don’t think I can do— You’re on tape now, sir! So— Miss whatever-the-hell-your-name-is. [unclear] Now! What I’m gonna ask you again… You’re already fired! But if you don’t wanna get me the names and the contact numbers of James Burton and Tom Hopkins, that’s up to you. Are you gonna do it or not?
No member here by that name!
Well then, I’m gonna tell you somethin’: there will be no employee by your name, either.

This is one of the greatest actors of all time. Greatest salesperson of all time! And you’ll soon be seeing me on… TV and motion pictures… all over the world![whispers]Thank you! Thank you!Thank you for the… great applause!
